Data Processing Addendum between Azure Lotus Holdings Limited (a Hong Kong listed issuer) as Controller and Verdant Crane Capital Management Ltd. as Processor, governed by Hong Kong law and the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, with personal data defined as data relating to a living individual whose identity is directly or indirectly ascertainable.
Our Data Protection Officer is Mr. R.K. Yuen, reachable at privacy@verdantcrane.example.hk; the Controller’s contact is Ms. E. Poon, Company Secretary, at cosec@azurelotus.example.com.hk.
Processing purposes include merger and acquisition diligence, fund investor onboarding (AML/KYC), and employment/secondment workflows, limited to what is necessary and proportionate for the above.
Processor may receive information constituting “inside information” under Hong Kong securities legislation (including Part XIVA concepts) and must keep such information confidential, avoid selective disclosure, and use it solely for agreed purposes.
Selective disclosure is permitted only under the incomplete negotiations safe harbour and solely to recipients subject to written confidentiality obligations; the Controller will make an announcement as soon as reasonably practicable once confidentiality no longer applies or price sensitivity arises, and the Processor shall not trade or recommend trading in the Controller’s securities.
Processor will observe blackout windows and closed periods notified by the Controller (including results blackout and any ad hoc trading restrictions), and will implement an internal dealing code for Access Persons mirroring the Controller’s code.
Prior to any onward transfer to sub-processors (including offshore affiliates), the Processor will confirm whether the relevant information has been “cleansed” by public announcement; for example, the Controller’s 3 May 2026 announcement on the Exchange’s news website (PHEX ref: P2026/1456) regarding the proposed acquisition of Coral Ridge Foods Limited is already public and should no longer be treated as MNPI.
Public materials such as the Controller’s FY2019 press release about a legacy joint venture and mainstream media summaries are stale and not, by themselves, indicative of current inside information status.
Non-issuer operational notices (e.g., Mayfly Facilities’ monthly maintenance memo for a third-party data room) are not inside information of the Controller and should not trigger trading halts or cleansing.
Subtle controls: if the audit timetable shifts a Board meeting into an earlier week, the Controller may declare an extended closed period; the Processor must update its watch/restricted lists within one business day of notice.
Personal data rights: the Processor will assist the Controller in responding to data access/erasure/rectification requests under Hong Kong privacy law within 40 days; DSARs received directly will be forwarded to the Controller without undue delay.
Cross-border transfers: until any statutory cross-border transfer provisions are commenced, the Processor will apply contractual safeguards and transfer impact assessments for exports to Verdant Crane Processing Hub (New Pearl City) and any other sub-processor, and will not export identity documents unless strictly necessary.
Retention: MNPI will be segregated and, once cleansed by a public announcement, reclassified and redacted in working papers within 30 days; personal data in diligence files will be retained no longer than 7 years from deal completion or engagement end, subject to legal hold.
Incident response: suspected personal data breaches or inadvertent disclosure of inside information will be notified to the Controller within 24 hours, with facts sufficient for the Controller to consider a trading halt and an “as soon as reasonably practicable” announcement; Processor will cooperate with any regulatory queries.
This Addendum does not cover marketing communications; for clarity, Verdant Crane will never request payment card PINs or internet banking passwords from data subjects.
